task_completion.description: Open the color-stroop.pbl file in a text editor.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor window (GUI or terminal-based) is open, and the file name 'color-stroop.pbl' is visible in the title bar, tab, or status bar.

task_completion.description: Locate the custompractrials and customtrials variables within the script.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text editor displays the section of the code where the 'custompractrials' and 'customtrials' variables are defined.

task_completion.description: Modify the number of practice trials to 16.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The code line defining 'custompractrials' has been changed so that the assigned value is 16 (e.g., 'custompractrials <- 16' or 'custompractrials = 16').

task_completion.description: Modify the number of test trials per block to 56.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The code line defining 'customtrials' has been changed so that the assigned value is 56 (e.g., 'customtrials <- 56' or 'customtrials = 56').

task_completion.description: Save the changes to the color-stroop.pbl file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text editor tab or status bar for 'color-stroop.pbl' does not show an unsaved changes indicator (such as a dot or asterisk), or a terminal message confirms the file was successfully written/saved.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used an interactive text editor rather than automated terminal commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show an interactive text editor interface (like VS Code, gedit, nano, or vim) being used to edit the file, rather than automated stream editors like 'sed' or 'echo' in a standard terminal prompt.

integrity.description: Verify the agent edited the actual existing script rather than creating a new dummy file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text editor shows the full PEBL script with its original surrounding code, functions, and comments, not just a blank file containing only the two modified variables.

integrity.description: Verify the rest of the script was not accidentally deleted or overwritten.
integrity.visual_evidence: Surrounding code and comments above and below the modified variables in the 'color-stroop.pbl' file remain visible and intact.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target file is 'color-stroop.pbl'. The default values in the original source code are custompractrials = 8 (or <- 8) and customtrials = 28 (or <- 28). The task requires modifying these specific variables to 16 and 56, respectively. PEBL scripts may use either '<-' or '=' for assignment.
